data.body:
PASS-2 INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION (findings 0–11). Re-grounded against attest.py:80–115 (claim_gate_check + build_manifest 33–41), __init__.py:75–82 (head_sha recorded separately from signed manifest), and ticket graph (epic 4fcd; children 22bf/5a6c; sibling-bug a36b). Verdicts with citations; no fixes.

[0] material-drift definition deferred. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=medium, blast=module, likelihood=high, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes — epic body says only "e.g. the resolved edit-set / cited files," makes no decision on authoritative signal or what "material" means; no_existing_mitigation=no — child 22bf §What/§Scope DECIDES it (dependency set = file_impact ∪ REVIEW_RESULT citations, whole-file content-hash; "material" = per-file content-hash mismatch/deletion); severity_claim_justified=insufficient; cited_reference_accurate=na. The epic body defers; the design-of-record child resolves.

[1] invalidate-OR-flag undecided. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=low, blast=module, likelihood=medium, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes (epic SC + AC both phrase as "invalidated (or flagged stale)" / "invalidates or flags"); no_existing_mitigation=no — child 22bf commits to BLOCK/invalidate ("Any mismatch … = drift → block the claim"); cited_reference_accurate=na. Genuine epic-body ambiguity; resolved at child altitude.

[2] "cheap enough not to tax hot paths" unbounded. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=low, blast=local, likelihood=medium, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes at epic altitude; no_existing_mitigation=no — child 22bf AC: "sub-ms for a handful of files; no claim hot-path regression"; the claim path (attest.py claim_gate_check, a pure-local check) is the documented hot path; cited_reference_accurate=na.

[3] ticket-event binding vs new code-grounding signal coexistence. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=low, blast=module, likelihood=medium, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes (epic body does not describe how the two bindings compose); no_existing_mitigation=no — attest.py:96–115 shows both gates already coexist (head_sha freshness then material fingerprint), and child 22bf states it replaces the step-3 whole-HEAD match while material fingerprint stays for ticket edits; cited_reference_accurate=na. Real epic-body terminology gap; mechanically resolved in child.

[4] rollout treatment of already-signed attestations / back-out. ATTRS: prod_impact=medium, debt=low, blast=system, likelihood=medium, reversibility=moderate. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes at epic altitude (epic body silent on retroactive-vs-grandfathered + false-invalidation back-out); no_existing_mitigation=insufficient — 22bf's empty-set conservative fallback and a36b address false-invalidation friction, but neither states rollout treatment of pre-feature signed attestations explicitly; path_reachable=yes; cited_reference_accurate=na. Genuine epic-scope residual.

[5] no ADR/CLAUDE.md/design-doc artifact named. ATTRS: prod_impact=none, debt=medium, blast=module, likelihood=medium, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes (epic body names no doc artifact); no_existing_mitigation=insufficient — child 5a6c AC requires "Descope is documented in code/docs" and 22bf updates the det_floor docstring (partial doc commitments for sub-parts), but no architectural-decision doc for the drift-binding decision is named anywhere. cited reference ("AC1") is an accurate paraphrase; cited_reference_accurate=na (no specific code line cited).

[6] configurability of drift threshold/scope unstated. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=low, blast=module, likelihood=low, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes (neither epic nor children state whether the dependency-set scope / "material" rule is configurable vs hardcoded); no_existing_mitigation=yes (no configurability commitment found); severity_claim_justified=insufficient — the threshold is content-hash exact-match, not a tunable magnitude, so "likely to evolve" is weaker than for a numeric threshold; cited_reference_accurate=na.

[7] AC2 couples correctness + perf without sizing/sequencing. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=low, blast=module, likelihood=low, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=partial at epic altitude; no_existing_mitigation=no — child 22bf carries file_impact = 3 named files + explicit perf AC ("sub-ms … no claim hot-path regression"); child 5a6c carries a measurement AC; the epic IS decomposed into a risk-ordered Story1→Story2 sequence (depends_on) per the decomposition comment. The sizing/sequencing requested exists at child altitude; cited_reference_accurate=na.

[8] premise "code drift is silent" contradicted by shipped head_sha binding; real residual is over-invalidation. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=medium, blast=module, likelihood=high, reversibility=easy. cited_reference_accurate=YES — VERIFIED attest.py:96–102 recomputes signing.head_sha and returns verdict "stale-head" when result['head_sha'] != head, so a code commit with no ticket edit already invalidates. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes (epic Context frames "silently staling"; shipped behavior is the inverse — over-invalidation); no_existing_mitigation=insufficient — child 22bf §Why AND sibling-bug a36b name the over-invalidation precisely, and THIS epic already carries G6 review comments stating exactly this reframing, yet the epic Context/Success-Criteria BODY still reads as under-invalidation; impact_follows_necessarily=yes. Strong, code-grounded finding; residual lives in children + comments, not the implementer-facing epic body.

[9] no positive rationale for content-addressed dependency-scope in epic body. ATTRS: prod_impact=none, debt=low, blast=module, likelihood=high, reversibility=easy. cited_reference_accurate=YES — VERIFIED build_manifest (attest.py:33–41) omits head_sha from the signed manifest; head_sha is attached as separate audit metadata in the signature record (__init__.py:81), confirming the "latent integrity gap" claim. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes (epic body states only problem + detect→invalidate/flag→tested; the content-addressed/Bazel/Gerrit rationale lives only in the decomposition comment + child 22bf §Why); no_existing_mitigation=insufficient — rationale exists, just not in the epic body, and a G6 review comment on THIS epic already raised it (largely a duplicate of a self-surfaced finding).

[10] (G3) child 5a6c does not cover parent AC1; correctly scoped to post-detection remedy. ATTRS: prod_impact=none, debt=none, blast=local, likelihood=low, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes — the finding's own assertion is that this is NOT a coverage gap (sibling 22bf fully owns AC1; 5a6c optimizes the remedy on top of Story-1 detection). VERIFIED: 22bf AC binds a signed {path: content-hash} map and removes the whole-HEAD match (owns AC1); 5a6c depends_on 22bf and re-runs only {P2,P3} ∪ CODEBASE_GROUNDED. The 4-bucket assignment (structural/orthogonal to AC1, no uncovered criterion) is correct; no_existing_mitigation=na; cited_reference_accurate=yes (both sibling ACs quoted accurately).

[11] (G4) scope overlap a36b ↔ 22bf over the same scoped-freshness behavior. ATTRS: prod_impact=low, debt=low, blast=module, likelihood=medium, reversibility=easy. is_verifiable=yes; evidence_entails_finding=yes — VERIFIED: a36b AC1 scopes freshness to "resolved edit-set / cited files rather than the whole HEAD sha" over attest.py claim_gate_check; 22bf description says "fixes worm-folly-barge" and 22bf AC2 re-specifies the same behavior ("Claim SUCCEEDS when an unrelated file changed … claim is BLOCKED when a dependency file's content changed"). Both target src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py claim_gate_check whole-HEAD-match removal — genuine duplicate/overlapping ownership of one behavior across two siblings. no_existing_mitigation=insufficient — a G4 container-review comment ALREADY on a36b proposes making it a non-implementing bug that 22bf "fixes," so the overlap is flagged but not yet resolved in the ticket structure; cited_reference_accurate=yes (all four quotes match the live tickets).

Method note: findings 8 and 9 cite accurate, verified code (attest.py:96–102; build_manifest 33–41 + __init__.py:81). Findings 0–4 are genuine epic-body gaps largely resolved in the design-of-record children (0–3) or a genuine epic-scope residual (4). 10 and 11 are accurate structural/overlap observations consistent with the live ticket graph; 11's overlap is real and only partially mitigated by an existing review comment.
